displayName: United Arab Emirates – Visitor Visa (Tourist / Short Stay)
shortDescription: Dubai · Sharjah · Abu Dhabi · 30/60/90 days · Single & multiple entry · India route

policyAlert.summary: Visitor visa fees vary by emirate, duration, and entry type. Confirm variant before quoting.

alert.title: Select correct duration & entry type
alert.body: Dubai 30/60/90 day single vs multiple entry have different government and consultancy fees. Sharjah and Abu Dhabi variants use separate sponsor channels.

about.label: Description
about.value: Short-stay visitor/tourist visa for UAE via Dubai, Sharjah, or Abu Dhabi routes from India. Available in 30, 60, and 90-day durations with single or multiple entry. Applied through authorised sponsor/typing centre or ICP e-visa where eligible.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Tourism, family visit, or short business trip · Valid passport · Sponsor or authorised agent · Return travel plan · No UAE employment intent on visitor visa

about.label: Dubai variants
about.value: 30/60/90 days — single entry and multiple entry (see Fees tab for Future Link consultancy by variant).

about.label: Sharjah & Abu Dhabi
about.value: Separate emirate sponsor routes with comparable duration tiers — consultancy from ₹4,500–7,500.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: ICP UAE · GDRFA Dubai · Sharjah / Abu Dhabi immigration · Authorised UAE sponsor or agent

about.label: After approval
about.value: Travel within visa validity; respect entry type and duration; overstay fines apply (AED 50/day approx. — verify current rate).

eligibility.criterion: Passport scan and photo per spec
Confirmed duration and entry type (30/60/90, single/multiple)
Emirate route selected (Dubai / Sharjah / Abu Dhabi)
No prior UAE overstay or ban

redFlagsBanner: Wrong entry type or emirate route is the most common rework — confirm variant before payment.

redFlags.title: Wrong variant purchased
redFlags.description: Needed multiple entry but bought single.
redFlags.fix: Match variant to travel plan before filing

faqs.q: Difference between single and multiple entry?
faqs.a: Single allows one entry during validity; multiple allows re-entries within validity period — confirm sponsor terms.

faqs.q: Can visitor visa be extended?
faqs.a: Extensions may be possible in UAE through sponsor — verify current GDRFA/ICP rules; do not overstay.

faqs.q: Dubai vs Sharjah visa?
faqs.a: Must match intended emirate of stay and sponsor; not interchangeable for all purposes.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: Short-stay visitor/tourist visa for UAE via Dubai, Sharjah, or Abu Dhabi routes from India. Available in 30, 60, and 90-day durations with single or multiple entry. Applied through authorised sponsor/typing centre or ICP e-visa where eligible.

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: Tourism, family visit, or short business trip · Valid passport · Sponsor or authorised agent · Return travel plan · No UAE employment intent on visitor visa

faqs.q: What is Dubai variants for this service?
faqs.a: 30/60/90 days — single entry and multiple entry (see Fees tab for Future Link consultancy by variant).

faqs.q: What is Sharjah & Abu Dhabi for this service?
faqs.a: Separate emirate sponsor routes with comparable duration tiers — consultancy from ₹4,500–7,500.

faqs.q: What is Key authority for this service?
faqs.a: ICP UAE · GDRFA Dubai · Sharjah / Abu Dhabi immigration · Authorised UAE sponsor or agent

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: Travel within visa validity; respect entry type and duration; overstay fines apply (AED 50/day approx. — verify current rate).

faqs.q: Is Valid passport (6+ months) required?
faqs.a: Valid passport (6+ months) is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Passport scan and photo per spec required?
faqs.a: Passport scan and photo per spec is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Confirmed duration and entry type (30/60/90, single/multiple) required?
faqs.a: Confirmed duration and entry type (30/60/90, single/multiple) is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Emirate route selected (Dubai / Sharjah / Abu Dhabi) required?
faqs.a: Emirate route selected (Dubai / Sharjah / Abu Dhabi) is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Return/onward travel plan required?
faqs.a: Return/onward travel plan is typically required.

faqs.q: Is No prior UAE overstay or ban required?
faqs.a: GDRFA history check

faqs.q: Is Sponsor or authorised agent engaged required?
faqs.a: Sponsor or authorised agent engaged is typically required.

faqs.q: Can Future Link guarantee approval?
faqs.a: No. Government authorities decide each case. Never promise approval.

faqs.q: What documents should we collect first?
faqs.a: Passport, application forms, fees, and the service checklist in the Checklist tab.

faqs.q: How long does processing take?
faqs.a: See KPI processing time — always verify current GDRFA/ICP timelines before quoting.

faqs.q: What are government fees?
faqs.a: Confirm latest fees on the official portal in the Resources section before quoting.

faqs.q: Can the client apply from India?
faqs.a: Yes — standard route for Indian nationals via sponsor, VFS, or ICP e-visa as applicable.

faqs.q: What if the client was refused before?
faqs.a: Disclose all refusals, overstays, and bans; address GDRFA fines before reapply.

faqs.q: Can the client work on this visa?
faqs.a: Work rights depend on visa type — employment visa required for full-time work.

faqs.q: United Arab Emirates – Visitor Visa (Tourist / Short Stay): counselor verification point 24?
faqs.a: Confirm eligibility, checklist sign-off, current fees, and client consent before submission.

faqs.q: United Arab Emirates – Visitor Visa (Tourist / Short Stay): counselor verification point 25?
faqs.a: Confirm eligibility, checklist sign-off, current fees, and client consent before submission.

faqs.a: Confirm eligibility, checklist sign-off, current fees, and client consent before submission.

faqs.q: United Arab Emirates – Visitor Visa (Tourist / Short Stay): counselor verification point 30?
faqs.a: Confirm eligibility, checklist sign-off, current fees, and client consent before submission.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
Verify licensed institution or employer before collecting full fees
proTips: Confirm 30 vs 60 vs 90 days before payment
Multiple entry costs more — use only if client needs re-entry
postApproval: Remind to carry insurance and accommodation proof at border
Note reapplication if travel plans change
